reasoning: The condition requires more than 10 times the baseline value of (1 + 30-day average) for 'Violence against civilians'. The current baseline value is 2.667, meaning the 30-day average is 1.667 (which translates to roughly 20 events over the past year). To satisfy the criteria, Thailand would need over 26.67 (i.e., 27 or more) 'Violence against civilians' events in a single 30-day window. This would mean experiencing more civilian targeting events in one month than typically occur in an entire year. Our searches confirm that while there is an ongoing low-level insurgency in the Deep South, violence has not spiked anywhere near this 15x magnitude. For the nearest resolution dates, the 30-day windows are either already underway or starting imminently, and no 20+-event surges are occurring. Thus, the near-term probabilities are placed at the minimum allowable threshold of 5%. The probability naturally broadens out slightly for the later dates to factor in inherent tail risks or sudden security paradigm shifts, though it remains highly unlikely.
